task_completion.description: Verify that the consumer groups 'CRITICAL_APP_CG' and 'BATCH_REPORT_CG' were created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing SQL commands (e.g., DBMS_RESOURCE_MANAGER.CREATE_CONSUMER_GROUP) executed successfully for both 'CRITICAL_APP_CG' and 'BATCH_REPORT_CG', or the groups appearing in a GUI list of Oracle consumer groups.

task_completion.description: Verify that the resource plan named 'STABILITY_PLAN' was created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the successful execution of a CREATE_PLAN command for 'STABILITY_PLAN', or the plan visible in an Oracle management GUI.

task_completion.description: Verify that CPU allocations are set to 60% for CRITICAL_APP_CG, 20% for BATCH_REPORT_CG, and 20% for OTHER_GROUPS at Management Level 1.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing plan directives being created (DBMS_RESOURCE_MANAGER.CREATE_PLAN_DIRECTIVE) with 'mgmt_p1' values of 60, 20, and 20 for the respective groups, or a GUI configuration screen showing these exact percentages.

task_completion.description: Verify that a switch rule is configured for CRITICAL_APP_CG to switch to BATCH_REPORT_CG after 60 seconds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the plan directive for CRITICAL_APP_CG with 'switch_group' set to 'BATCH_REPORT_CG' and 'switch_time' (or execution time limit) set to 60 seconds.

task_completion.description: Verify that APP_USER is mapped to CRITICAL_APP_CG, RPT_USER is mapped to BATCH_REPORT_CG, and both are granted switch privileges.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing SET_CONSUMER_GROUP_MAPPING commands mapping the users to their respective groups, and GRANT_SWITCH_PRIVILEGE commands executed successfully for both users.

task_completion.description: Verify that 'STABILITY_PLAN' is enabled as the active resource plan.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the execution of 'ALTER SYSTEM SET RESOURCE_MANAGER_PLAN = STABILITY_PLAN' or a GUI setting applying the plan to the system.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent interacted with a genuine Oracle Database client (e.g., SQL*Plus, SQL Developer, or Enterprise Manager).
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface shown in the screenshots should be a real database terminal or GUI, not a generic text editor or mock environment.

integrity.description: Verify that the configuration commands were actually executed and not just typed out.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for success confirmation messages from the database, such as 'PL/SQL procedure successfully completed.', 'System altered.', or GUI success dialogs.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent did not simply copy-paste the expected output or task description into a file to fake completion.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal or GUI history should show a logical sequence of commands and database responses, rather than a static text file containing the task requirements.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Plan Name: STABILITY_PLAN. Consumer Groups: CRITICAL_APP_CG, BATCH_REPORT_CG. Users: APP_USER, RPT_USER. CPU Allocations: CRITICAL_APP_CG (60%), BATCH_REPORT_CG (20%), OTHER_GROUPS (20%) at Management Level 1. Switch Rule: CRITICAL_APP_CG switches to BATCH_REPORT_CG if a call exceeds 60 seconds.
